title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (April 1)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: A one-family Cape Cod home at 42 Lawsbrook Road, Acton, sold for $530,000. It features 3 bedrooms and 2 baths.
In Allston, a two-family home at 65-67 Adamson St., built in 1910, sold for $1,200,000. It includes 4 bedrooms and 2 baths.
In Boston, a condo at 682 Atlantic Ave. sold for the highest price of $14,131,000.

title: Drake Maye surprises fan wearing ‘I [Heart] Drake Maye’ t-shirt at Bruins game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Drake and Ann Michael Maye attended the Boston Bruins game against the Dallas Stars at TD Garden.
Drake Maye saluted a fan wearing an 'I [Heart] Drake Maye' t-shirt, creating a viral moment during the game.
Maye participated in Granite Telecommunications' 'Saving by Shaving' event, raising over $4 million for Boston Children's Hospital.

title: Former Celtics coach Doc Rivers reportedly voted into Basketball Hall of Fame
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Former Celtics coach Doc Rivers has been voted into the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame's Class of 2026.
Rivers led the Boston Celtics to an NBA championship in 2008 and coached the team from 2004-2013.
Rivers, a 64-year-old coach with a 1,191-861 record, is one of 11 NBA coaches with over 1,000 wins.

title: Drake Maye shaves his head as part of charity event benefiting Boston Children’s Hospital
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Drake Maye shaved his head for the Granite Telecommunications 'Saving by Shaving' event, raising funds for Boston Children's Hospital.
The event raised $4.15 million, with Granite Telecommunications donating $2,500 per person who shaved their head or donated hair.
Drake and Ann Michael Maye recently launched the 'MayeDay Family Foundation' to support children and families in need.

title: Boston police officer criminally charged after allegedly leaving loaded gun unsecured in vehicle
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston police officer Sean Cullen charged with improper storage of firearm after leaving loaded gun unsecured in vehicle.
Cullen placed on administrative leave and his law enforcement certification suspended following felony charge.
Incident stemmed from a domestic dispute at Cullen's West Roxbury home in December, leading to discovery of unsecured weapons.

title: N.H. governor’s security aid rescues Mass. driver from fiery toll booth crash
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mass. driver seriously injured in crash at Bedford Toll Plaza on the Everett Turnpike, vehicle caught fire.
N.H. Gov. Kelly Ayotte and her security trooper helped rescue the trapped driver from the flaming car.
Traffic rerouted as investigators closed tolls; lanes mostly reopened, crash under investigation by N.H. State Police.

title: Mike Vrabel talks A.J. Brown, Patriots draft, and other takeaways from NFL owners’ meetings
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Mike Vrabel discusses Patriots' strategies for acquiring star receiver A.J. Brown, suggesting potential post-June 1 trade with Eagles.
Patriots aim for championship in 2026, with coach Vrabel emphasizing the importance of playoff success and hosting games.
New England plans for 2026 NFL Draft, focusing on best player available and potential tight end acquisition as a priority.

title: Mass. man charged with murdering wife, allegedly told 911 operator ‘my gun went off’
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Mark Opper, 56, from Belchertown, is charged with murdering his wife after allegedly shooting her in their home.
Police were called to the scene where they found Diane Opper with multiple gunshot wounds; she later died at the hospital.
Mark Opper is being held without bail and is scheduled for a court appearance on April 27 for a probable cause hearing.

title: Brockton man facing 14 charges after allegedly engaging in shootout with police
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jailson Andrade, 44, was arraigned on 14 charges after a shootout with Brockton police officers during a traffic stop.
Andrade allegedly fired at officers after a police chase; he was shot in the shoulder and subdued by police.
The shooting investigation is ongoing; Andrade has a criminal record including assaults, and is held without bail.

title: 1 killed, 1 injured in Charlton crash, driver reportedly refused to stop for police
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A fatal crash occurred on Route 20 in Charlton, killing an SUV driver and injuring another individual.
The crash involved a pick-up truck that refused to stop for police, resulting in a head-on collision.
Authorities are investigating the incident, with Worcester County DA's office involved in the process.
